meaning: adj.浣跨牬瑁傜殑,鍒嗚鎬х殑
examples: Alcohol can produce violent, disruptive behaviour.
Overfishing can be seriously disruptive to coral.
The crisis will prove hugely disruptive, however.
So a whole generation of people moving abroad is disruptive to families, as well as the economy.
Or are they doomed to decline and die, eventually to be replaced by disruptive new upstarts?
